text: With great power comes great responsibility.
context: Uncle Ben's dying words to Peter Parker, establishing the moral core of Spider-Man's character across nearly every adaptation in film, television, and comics.

text: You have a gift. A power that most people will never understand. And with that power comes a responsibility. A responsibility to use it to help people, to protect them, not to hurt them.
context: The full context of the responsibility lesson, emphasizing protection over personal gain.

text: I can't just stand by and watch bad things happen. I have to do something. I have to be better.
context: Peter's realization that ignoring his power makes him responsible for the consequences.

text: With great power comes great responsibility. Don't ever forget that.
context: The direct, memorable phrasing that has echoed through generations of Spider-Man stories.

text: It's not who I am underneath, but what I do that defines me.
context: Batman’s mentor Ra's al Ghul and Bruce's own realization about actions defining character and responsibility.

text: All we have to decide is what to do with the time that is given us.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001)
context: Gandalf reminding Frodo that even in dark times, individuals bear responsibility for their choices and actions.

text: It is our choices, Harry, that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.
source: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets (2002)
context: Albus Dumbledore teaching Harry that personal responsibility and moral choices define a person more than innate talent or power.

text: The needs of the many outweigh the needs of the few, or the one.
source: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan (1982)
context: Spock's logical yet profoundly responsible decision to sacrifice himself for the greater good of the crew and mission.

source: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King (2003)
context: Éowyn defying prophecy and societal expectations to take responsibility for defending her people in battle.

text: You have to do what you think is right, no matter what anyone else says.
context: Peter grappling with the heavy responsibility of his powers and the difficult choices they force upon him.

text: If you could do good things for other people, you had a moral obligation to do those things.
context: Tony reinforcing the Spider-Man responsibility ethos to a young Peter struggling with the weight of his abilities.

text: The only way to deal with an unfree world is to become so absolutely free that your very existence is an act of rebellion.
context: A quote often associated with themes of personal responsibility versus institutional duty in the series.
